_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to describe and use protective behaviours and help-seeking strategies for both online and offline situations.

_source.w: We are learning to describe and use protective behaviours and help-seeking strategies for both online and offline situations.
y: Protective behaviours keep us safe in all environments — including online.
Knowing help-seeking strategies means we are never stuck dealing with a problem alone.
These skills are increasingly important as we spend more time in digital spaces.
eg: Discussing what to do if someone online asks for personal information.
Identifying trusted adults we can go to with online or offline worries.
Practising how to report inappropriate content or behaviour online.
Role-playing protective behaviours in a scenario involving peer pressure.
Creating a safety plan for both online and offline situations.
Discussing the difference between helpful and harmful online interactions.
